etymology.description: semantic 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 立(立) (lì) -> pull
etymology.interpretation: The character 拉 (lā, "pull; drag; draw out") combines 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") as the semantic element, suggesting an action performed with the hand, with 立(立) (lì) as the phonetic component, providing the 'lā' sound. The intuitive image is a hand performing the action of pulling or dragging.

examples.chinese: 请帮我把椅子拉过来(請幫我把椅子拉過來)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ bǎ yǐzi lā guòlái.
examples.english: Please help me pull the chair over.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢在地上拉着玩具车(他喜歡在地上拉著玩具車)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài dìshàng lā zhe wánjùchē.
examples.english: He likes to drag toy cars on the floor.

examples.chinese: 我们要用力把船拉到岸边(我們要用力把船拉到岸邊)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào yònglì bǎ chuán lā dào ànbiān.
examples.english: We need to pull the boat to the shore with force.
